questions.question: What is the topic for Week 14?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Fourteen is PEER PRESSURE.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 12?
questions.explanation: Dominion through Prayers was the topic for Week 12.

questions.question: Do not be deceived: 'Evil company corrupts good ________'
questions.explanation: The memory verse (1 Corinthians 15:33) ends with 'corrupts good habits'.

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The reference for the memory verse is 1 Corinthians 15:33.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 14 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: Do not be deceived: “Evil company corrupts good habits”
questions.explanation: Full text recall of 1 Corinthians 15:33.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (1 Corinthians 15:33), what does evil company corrupt?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states 'Evil company corrupts good habits'.

questions.question: How are 'peers' defined in the introduction of the Week 14 lesson?
options: A. Enemies who want to destroy you
B. Mentors who are older than you
C. Individuals within the same social group
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Peers are individuals within the same social group...'

questions.question: According to the introduction, peer pressure is especially dangerous for those whose personal convictions are fully developed.
questions.explanation: It says it is dangerous 'especially for those whose personal convictions are NOT yet fully developed or established.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'IDENTIFYING PEER PRESSURE'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, what must individuals develop to resist negative peer influence?
options: A. A large group of friends
B. Strong convictions and a clear sense of identity
questions.explanation: The text says 'Individuals must develop strong convictions and a clear sense of identity to resist negative peer influence.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding the pressure to engage in 'Indecent Dressing' and worldly fashion standards?
questions.explanation: The text links this to '(Romans 12:2)'.

questions.question: Which king was misled by King Ahab into a dangerous battle, used as an example of misleading peer influence?
questions.explanation: Point 3 states 'Just as King Jehoshaphat was misled by King Ahab into a dangerous battle...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show the danger of the 'Pursuit of Wealth through Unethical Means'?
questions.explanation: The text links this to '(1 Timothy 6:10)'.

questions.question: Influence of Ungodly Mentors is one of the examples of peer pressure listed in Outline I.
questions.explanation: Point 4 explicitly states 'Influence of Ungodly Mentors'.

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'STRATEGIES TO STAND FIRM'.

questions.question: To resist peer pressure, the Week 14 lesson says it is essential to grow spiritually and allow what to guide you effectively?
questions.explanation: The text states 'allow the Spirit of God to guide you effectively (2 Peter 3:18; Isaiah 30:21).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support the strategy to 'Prepare in Advance'?
questions.explanation: Point 2 lists 'Prepare in Advance... (Genesis 39:9-13).'

questions.question: What does the strategy 'Establish a Secret Code' entail?
options: A. Speaking in tongues when pressured
B. Using a secret handshake to identify true believers
C. Creating a way to communicate with a trusted adult if you need help exiting a situation
D. Writing coded messages to friends
questions.explanation: Point 3 states 'Create a way to communicate with a trusted adult, such as a parent or mentor, if you need help exiting a situation.'

questions.question: According to the strategy 'Practice Saying No', how should you say no to peer pressure?
options: B. Firmly say no without apologising or offering explanations
C. Try to explain your religious views in detail
D. Say maybe first, then later say no
questions.explanation: Point 4 states 'Firmly say no without apologising or offering explanations (James 5:12).'

questions.question: Which verse is cited for 'Surround Yourself with Supportive Friends'?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'Surround Yourself with Supportive Friends... (Proverbs 13:20).'

questions.question: Offering positive alternatives that steer clear of trouble is one of the strategies to stand firm.
questions.explanation: Point 7 explicitly states 'Offer Alternatives: Suggest positive activities that steer clear of trouble.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support the strategy to 'Be Honest' about how the pressure makes you feel?
questions.explanation: Point 8 lists 'Be Honest... (2 Kings 2:3).'

questions.question: According to the conclusion, by focusing on building yourself spiritually, emotionally, and mentally, you equip yourself to positively ________ others.
questions.explanation: The conclusion says 'you equip yourself to positively impact others.'

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (2 Chronicles 10:3-11), who came to speak to King Rehoboam regarding the heavy yoke?
questions.explanation: Verse 3 says 'And Jeroboam and all Israel came and spoke to Rehoboam'.

questions.question: What request did Jeroboam and Israel make to King Rehoboam?
options: A. To give them more land
B. To lighten the burdensome service and heavy yoke his father put on them
C. To go to war with Syria
D. To build a new temple
questions.explanation: Verse 4 says 'now therefore, lighten the burdensome service of your father and his heavy yoke which he put on us...'

questions.question: When King Rehoboam consulted the elders, what advice did they give him? (2 Chronicles 10:7)
options: A. If you are kind, please them, and speak good words to them, they will be your servants forever
B. Do not listen to them; show your strength
D. Tell them to return after forty days
questions.explanation: Verse 7 says 'If you are kind to these people, and please them, and speak good words to them, they will be your servants forever.'

questions.question: After rejecting the elders' advice, who did Rehoboam consult next?
options: C. The young men who had grown up with him
D. The priests of the temple
questions.explanation: Verse 8 says 'and consulted the young men who had grown up with him'.

questions.question: What did the young men advise Rehoboam to say about his 'little finger' compared to his father? (2 Chronicles 10:10)
options: A. My little finger shall be thicker than my father’s waist
B. My little finger is stronger than my father's arm
C. My little finger holds more wealth than my father's kingdom
D. My little finger is wiser than my father's head
questions.explanation: Verse 10 states 'My little finger shall be thicker than my father’s waist!'

questions.question: What did the young men advise Rehoboam to say about how he would chastise the people? (2 Chronicles 10:11)
questions.explanation: Verse 11 says 'my father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scourges!'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'So he said to them, “Come back to me after three days.” And the people departed.'
questions.verse_text: So he said to them, “Come back to me after three days.” And the people departed.
questions.explanation: This is verse 5 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to Outline II, 'Seek Guidance' means you should keep your challenges to yourself and not talk to anyone.
questions.explanation: Point 6 says 'Talk about your challenges with a trusted person who can offer support and advice'.

questions.question: Why is peer pressure considered particularly dangerous according to the introduction?
options: A. Because it can get you arrested
B. Because it can strongly influence behaviour, especially for those whose personal convictions are not fully developed
C. Because friends always give bad advice
D. Because it only happens in schools
questions.explanation: The text states 'peer pressure is particularly dangerous because it can strongly influence behaviour, especially for those whose personal convictions are not yet fully developed'.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support the strategy to 'Believe in Yourself and Your Values' by daring to walk away?
questions.explanation: Point 1 cites '(Proverbs 1:10-14)' for this strategy.

questions.question: Which verse is cited along with 2 Peter 3:18 to support allowing the Spirit of God to guide you effectively?
questions.explanation: The text says 'allow the Spirit of God to guide you effectively (2 Peter 3:18; Isaiah 30:21).'

questions.question: the Week 14 lesson advises to spend time with friends who share your values and avoid those whose influence is harmful.
questions.explanation: Point 5 says 'Spend time with friends who share your values and avoid those whose influence is harmful'.
